metadata.description:
Gets the environment suffix for the Azure services.
Example usage, `getEnvironmentSuffix().storage => 'core.windows.net'`
Gives default azure builtin roles name to id mapping

Gets a deployment safe name by hashing a given deployment name and returning a deterministic unique string less than or equal to 64 characters.
Example usage, `getSafeDeploymentName('my-deployment-name') => 'D4BF8FFBFB96424CA07EE1E3E7BCA12Emy-deployment-name'`
The Application (Client) ID of the Compliance Monitoring SPN. Required when enableSoxCompliance is true, for Kusto cluster monitoring.
The Object (Principal) ID of the Compliance Monitoring SPN. Required when enableSoxCompliance is true, for role assignments on subscription and Synapse.

The Object (Principal) ID of the multi-tenant app authorized to push CDM data. Required when attachToCentralDatalake is false (data push mode). This app is granted Storage Blob Data Contributor on the deployed storage accounts.

source:
// The block is commented as the below values will come from synapse adf pipeline as runtime arguments.
// It is kept for debugging notebook for issues.
/*
val manifestFilePath = "abfss://config@<storageAccountName>.dfs.core.windows.net/manifest/v1.0/subscription-catalog.json"
val dataProductName = "Products"
val datasetName = "CDM_Products"
val cluster = "<kustoClusterName>"
val database = "Products"
val internalContainerPath = "abfss://internal@<storageAccountName>.dfs.core.windows.net"
val datasetVersion = "3.0"
*/
val environmentSuffix: Map[String, String] = Map(
  "storageEnvironmentSuffix" -> "<storageEnvironmentSuffix>",
  "sqlServerEnvironmentSuffix" -> "<sqlServerEnvironmentSuffix>",
  "frontdoorEnvironmentSuffix" -> "<frontdoorEnvironmentSuffix>",
  "keyvaultEnvironmentSuffix" -> "<keyvaultEnvironmentSuffix>"
)
println(s"manifestFilePath: $manifestFilePath, dataProductName: $dataProductName, datasetName: $datasetName, cluster: $cluster, database: $database , internalContainerPath: $internalContainerPath , datasetVersion: $datasetVersion")

// From the manifest file, fetch the index file and get the storage paths for the dataset
import scala.collection.mutable.ListBuffer
import org.apache.spark.sql.expressions.Window
import org.apache.spark.sql.functions._
import org.apache.spark.sql.types._

val manifestSchema: StructType =
  StructType(Seq(
    StructField("schema", StringType, true),
    StructField("version", StringType, true),
    StructField("subscriber",
      StructType(Seq(
        StructField("tenant_id", StringType, true),
        StructField("subscription_id", StringType, true),
        StructField("org_name", StringType, true),
        StructField("last_updated_at", StringType, true)
      )),
      true
    ),
    StructField("data_products",
      ArrayType(
        StructType(Seq(
          StructField("name", StringType, true),
          StructField("scenario_description", StringType, true),
          StructField("enabled", BooleanType, true),
          StructField("output_configuration",
            StructType(Seq(
              StructField("base_path", StringType, true),
              StructField("container", StringType, true),
              StructField("storage_account", StringType, true),
              StructField("additional_storage_accounts", ArrayType(StringType), true)
            )),
            true
          ),
          StructField("tables",
            ArrayType(
              StructType(Seq(
                StructField("name", StringType, true),
                StructField("version", StringType, true),
                StructField("sub_path", StringType, true)
              )),
              true
            ),
            true
          )
        )),
        true
      ),
      true
    )
  )
)

val dfManifest = spark.read.schema(manifestSchema).option("multiline", "true").json(manifestFilePath)

val datasetManifestEntryDf =
  dfManifest
    .select(explode($"data_products").as("dp"))
    .select(
      $"dp.name".as("dataProductName"),
      coalesce($"dp.enabled", lit(true)).as("isDataProductEnabled"),
      $"dp.output_configuration.storage_account".as("storage_account"),
      $"dp.output_configuration.container".as("container"),
      $"dp.output_configuration.base_path".as("base_path"),
      explode($"dp.tables").as("tables")
    )
    .filter(
      $"isDataProductEnabled" &&
      $"dataProductName" === dataProductName &&
      $"tables.name" === datasetName &&
      $"tables.version".cast("string") === datasetVersion
    )

if (datasetManifestEntryDf.head(1).isEmpty) {
  mssparkutils.notebook.exit(
    s"Manifest file is empty/unreadable: $manifestFilePath or $dataProductName is disabled for copy."
  )
}
val storage_suffix = environmentSuffix("storageEnvironmentSuffix")
val index_file_path: String =
  datasetManifestEntryDf
    .withColumn(
      "fullPath",
      concat(
        lit("abfss://"),
        $"container",
        lit("@"),
        $"storage_account",
        lit(".dfs."),
        lit(storage_suffix),
        $"base_path",
        lit(datasetName),
        coalesce($"tables.sub_path", lit("")),
        lit("/index-latest.csv")
      )
    )
    .select($"fullPath")
    .as[String]
    .head()

case class PartitionStorageInfo(partition: String, paths: Seq[String], runId: String)

val partitionStorageInfoList = ListBuffer[PartitionStorageInfo]()

// Non-partitioned dataset
if (!isPartitionedDataset) {
  println(s"$datasetName is not partitioned")

  val df = spark.read
    .option("header", "true")
    .csv(index_file_path)
    .select("Path", "Partition", "RunId")
    .orderBy(desc("RunId"))

  val latestRow = df.first()

  partitionStorageInfoList += PartitionStorageInfo(
    partition = latestRow.getAs[String]("Partition"),
    paths      = Seq(latestRow.getAs[String]("Path")),
    runId     = latestRow.getAs[String]("RunId") 
  )
}

// Partitioned dataset
if (isPartitionedDataset) {
  println(s"$datasetName is partitioned")

  val df = spark.read
    .option("header", "true")
    .csv(index_file_path)
    .filter(col("IngestionMode") === "Snapshot")
    .select("Path", "Partition", "RunId")

  val window = Window.partitionBy("Partition").orderBy(col("RunId").desc)

  val latestEntriesPerPartition =
    df.withColumn("rnk", rank().over(window))
      .filter(col("rnk") === 1)
      .drop("rnk")
  
  val latestGroupedEntriesPerPartition =
  latestEntriesPerPartition
    .groupBy(col("Partition"), col("RunId"))
    .agg(collect_set(col("Path")).as("Paths"))
  
  latestGroupedEntriesPerPartition
  .select(col("Partition"), col("Paths"), col("RunId"))
  .collect()
  .foreach { row =>
    val partition = row.getAs[String]("Partition")
    val paths     = row.getAs[Seq[String]]("Paths")
    val runId     = row.getAs[String]("RunId")
    partitionStorageInfoList += PartitionStorageInfo(partition = partition, paths = paths, runId = runId)
  }
}

val partitionListToBeLoaded: List[PartitionStorageInfo] = partitionStorageInfoList.toList
partitionListToBeLoaded.foreach { p =>
  println(s"------------------ Partition=${p.partition}, RunId=${p.runId}, Path=${p.paths.mkString(", ")} --------------------------------")

/*
1. Create dataset_STAGING and write into that
2. Write into dataset_STAGING table and validate the row count with storage
3. Create dataset_PROD table from dataset_STAGING table if it doesn't exists
4. Rename dataset_PROD table and dataset_STAGING table
5. Drop dataset_STAGING
6. Create view dataset on dataset_PROD

object Constants {
  val CONNECTION_TIMEOUT = 30
  val SOCKET_TIMEOUT = 30
  val CONNECTION_REQUEST_TIMEOUT = 5
  val MAX_NUMBER_OF_CONNECTIONS=200

import org.apache.spark.sql.{DataFrame, SparkSession}
import org.apache.spark.sql.expressions.Window
import org.apache.spark.sql.functions._
import org.apache.spark.sql.types._

object DataExploration {

  // Configuration case class to hold settings
  case class ExplorationConfig(
      manifestFilePath: String,
      dataProductName: String,
      datasetName: String,
      datasetVersion: String
  )

  /**
   * Retrieves the full path to the IndexLatest.csv file based on the manifest configuration.
   */
  def getIndexFilePath(spark: SparkSession, config: ExplorationConfig): String = {
    import spark.implicits._

    val dfManifest = spark.read.option("multiline", "true").json(config.manifestFilePath)

    val datasetManifestEntryDf =
      dfManifest
        .select(explode($"data_products").as("dp"))
        .select(
          $"dp.name".as("dataProductName"),
          coalesce($"dp.enabled", lit(true)).as("isDataProductEnabled"),
          $"dp.output_configuration.storage_account".as("storage_account"),
          $"dp.output_configuration.container".as("container"),
          $"dp.output_configuration.base_path".as("base_path"),
          explode($"dp.tables").as("tables")
        )
        .filter(
          $"isDataProductEnabled" &&
          $"dataProductName" === config.dataProductName &&
          $"tables.name" === config.datasetName &&
          $"tables.version".cast("string") === config.datasetVersion
        )

    if (datasetManifestEntryDf.head(1).isEmpty) {
      mssparkutils.notebook.exit(
        s"Manifest file is empty/unreadable: ${config.manifestFilePath} or ${config.dataProductName} is disabled for copy."
      )
    }

    datasetManifestEntryDf
      .withColumn(
        "fullPath",
        concat(
          lit("abfss://"),
          $"container",
          lit("@"),
          $"storage_account",
          lit(".dfs.<storageEnvironmentSuffix>"),
          $"base_path",
          lit(config.datasetName),
          coalesce($"tables.sub_path", lit("")),
          lit("/index-latest.csv")
        )
      )
      .select($"fullPath")
      .as[String]
      .head()
  }

  /**
   * Reads the index file and returns the list of paths for the latest snapshot.
   */
  def getSnapshotPaths(spark: SparkSession, indexFilePath: String): Array[String] = {
    import spark.implicits._
    
    val window = Window.partitionBy("Partition").orderBy(col("RunId").desc)
    println(indexFilePath)

    spark.read
      .option("header", "true")
      .csv(indexFilePath)
      .filter(col("IngestionMode") === "Snapshot")
      .withColumn("rnk", rank().over(window))
      .filter(col("rnk") === 1)
      .select("Path").distinct.as[String].collect()
  }

  /**
   * Loads the data from the provided paths into a DataFrame.
   */
  def loadData(spark: SparkSession, paths: Array[String]): DataFrame = {
    if (paths.isEmpty) {
      spark.emptyDataFrame
    } else {
      spark.read.parquet(paths: _*)
    }
  }

  /**
   * Helper method to execute the flow with the provided configuration.
   */
  def run(spark: SparkSession, config: ExplorationConfig): DataFrame = {
    val indexFilePath = getIndexFilePath(spark, config)
    val paths = getSnapshotPaths(spark, indexFilePath)
    loadData(spark, paths)
  }

data_products.name: Products
data_products.scenario_description: Canonical definitions of Microsoft and SDC offerings. Enables portfolio analysis and product recommendations.

data_products.name: Prices
data_products.scenario_description: Authoritative pricing data, including discounts and negotiated rates. Supports pricing analysis and forecasting.

data_products.name: Charges
data_products.scenario_description: Monetized usage and entitlement-based charges. Enables billing accuracy and operational efficiency insights.

data_products.name: Benefits
data_products.scenario_description: Tracks benefits, credits, and contractual obligations (e.g., reserved instances, savings plans). Supports retention forecasting.

data_products.name: CloudDimensions
data_products.scenario_description: Static and dynamic dimensions (e.g., geography, meter region, resource location). Supports contextual analysis.

variables.uploadInputParamsScriptContent:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ACCOUNT_NAME=""
CONTAINER_NAME=""
PAYLOAD_BASE64=""

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --accountName)
      ACCOUNT_NAME="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --container)
      CONTAINER_NAME="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --payload)
      PAYLOAD_BASE64="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
      exit 2
      ;;
  esac
done

if [[ -z "$ACCOUNT_NAME" || -z "$CONTAINER_NAME" || -z "$PAYLOAD_BASE64" ]]; then
  echo "Missing required args. Usage: --accountName <name> --container <name> --payload <base64>" >&2
  exit 2
fi

TIMESTAMP_UTC="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%MZ)"
BLOB_NAME="input-parameters/params-${TIMESTAMP_UTC}.json"

TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$TMP_FILE"' EXIT

echo "$PAYLOAD_BASE64" | base64 -d | jq '{
  "$schema": "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentParameters.json#",
  "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
  "parameters": (. | to_entries | map({key: .key, value: {value: .value}}) | from_entries)
}' > "$TMP_FILE"

az storage blob upload \
  --auth-mode login \
  --account-name "$ACCOUNT_NAME" \
  --container-name "$CONTAINER_NAME" \
  --name "$BLOB_NAME" \
  --file "$TMP_FILE" \
  --overwrite true \
  1>/dev/null

if [[ -n "${AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH:-}" ]]; then
  printf '{"storageAccount":"%s","container":"%s","blobName":"%s","timestampUtc":"%s"}' \
    "$ACCOUNT_NAME" "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$BLOB_NAME" "$TIMESTAMP_UTC" \
    > "$AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH"
fi
variables.synapseResourceCheckScript:
#!/bin/bash

set -e
set -o errexit
set -o nounset
set -o pipefail

resourceId=$1

# parse subscriptionId from resourceId
subscriptionId=$(echo $resourceId | cut -d'/' -f3)
resourceGroupName=$(echo $resourceId | cut -d'/' -f5)

# login
az login --identity
az account set --subscription $subscriptionId

# check if resource id exists in azure
exists=$(az resource list -g $resourceGroupName --query '[].id | contains(@, `'$resourceId'`)' -o tsv)

# Construct JSON output using jq
output=$(jq -n --arg resourceId "$resourceId" --arg exists "$exists" '{"resourceId": $resourceId, "exists": ($exists == "true")}')

echo $output >$AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH

variables.postDeploymentScriptContent:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Assign both Synapse Reader and Synapse Administrator roles to a UAMI

set -euo pipefail

# Parse JSON parameters
workspaceName=$(jq -r '.workspaceName' <<<"$@")
synapseAdminRoleId=$(jq -r '.synapseAdminRoleId' <<<"$@")
userAssignedMIPrincipalId=$(jq -r '.userAssignedMIPrincipalId' <<<"$@")
subscriptionId=$(jq -r '.subscriptionId' <<<"$@")
resourceGroup=$(jq -r '.resourceGroup' <<<"$@")
viewerGroupObjectId=$(jq -r '.viewerGroupObjectId' <<<"$@")
enableSox=$(jq -r '.enableSox' <<<"$@")
complianceMonitoringSPNObjectId=$(jq -r '.complianceMonitoringSPNObjectId' <<<"$@")

echo "Checking Synapse Administrator role assignment for UAMI: $userAssignedMIPrincipalId in workspace: $workspaceName"


role_cleanup(){
    #removing role assignments for dime uami
    local principalId="${userAssignedMIPrincipalId:-}"
    local subId="${subscriptionId:-}"
    local rg="${resourceGroup:-}"

    if [[ -z "$principalId" || -z "$subId" || -z "$rg" ]]; then
        echo "Skipping role cleanup (missing required inputs)"
        return 0
    fi

    echo "Removing Contributor and User Access Admin Temporary Role assignments"
    az role assignment delete --assignee "$principalId" --role "Contributor" --scope "/subscriptions/$subId/resourceGroups/$rg" -y || true
    az role assignment delete --assignee "$principalId" --role "User Access Administrator" --scope "/subscriptions/$subId/resourceGroups/$rg" -y || true

    echo "Removed Contributor and User Access Admin Temporary Role assignments"
}

trap 'role_cleanup' EXIT


# Allow time for Synapse role assignments to propagate
echo "Waiting 5 minutes for RBAC propagation..."
sleep 5m

assign_synapse_role() {
    local synapseWs="$1"
    local role="$2"
    local assigneeObjectId="$3"

    echo -e "Checking if $role role assignment exists for assignee ID: $assigneeObjectId\n"

    existingRoleAssignments=$(az synapse role assignment list --workspace-name "$synapseWs" --role "$role" --assignee-object-id "$assigneeObjectId" --query [].id -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo -e "Error: Failed to check existing role assignments.\n"
        return 1
    fi

    if [[ -z "$existingRoleAssignments" ]]; then
        echo -e "$role role assignment does not exist for assignee ID: $assigneeObjectId on $synapseWs \n"
        az synapse role assignment create --workspace-name "$synapseWs" --role "$role" --assignee-object-id "$assigneeObjectId"
        echo -e "$role role assignment created for assignee ID: $assigneeObjectId on $synapseWs \n"
        isSynapseRbacUpdated=1
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "$role role assignment already exists for assignee ID: $assigneeObjectId on $synapseWs \n"
        return 0
    fi
}

execute_with_retry() {
    set +e
    set +o pipefail

    local max_retries=15
    local delay=60
    local attempt=1

    while (( attempt <= max_retries )); do
        echo "Attempt $attempt: Executing command..."

        # Run the command with arguments
        "$@"

        if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
            echo "Attempt $attempt: Success"
            return 0
        else
            echo "Attempt $attempt: Command failed"
            if (( attempt < max_retries )); then
                echo "Retrying in $delay seconds..."
                sleep $delay
            fi
        fi

        (( attempt++ ))
    done

    echo "All retries failed."
    set -e
    set -o pipefail
    return 1
}

check_user_synapse_admin() {
    set +e
    echo "Checking if current user is Synapse Admin"
    stdout=$(mktemp)
    stderr=$(mktemp)
    az synapse role assignment list --workspace-name $workspaceName --role "6e4bf58a-b8e1-4cc3-bbf9-d73143322b78" > "$stdout" 2> "$stderr"
    # local response=$(az synapse role assignment list --workspace-name $workspaceName --role "6e4bf58a-b8e1-4cc3-bbf9-d73143322b78" --assignee-object-id $userAssignedMIPrincipalId)
    local error_message=$(cat "$stderr")
    synapse_response=$(cat "$stdout")

    local command_status=$?

    local is_admin=false

    if [[ $command_status -ne 0  || $error_message == *"Code: Unauthorized"* ]]; then
        echo "Synapse Role Assignment does not exist. Will need to elevate role"
        cat $stderr >&4
         set -e
         return 1
    else
        echo -e "$synapse_response" | jq . >/dev/null 2>&1
        local json_status=$?

        echo -e "JQ Parse Status: $json_status"
        echo -e "Response : $synapse_response"
        if [ $json_status -ne 0 ] || [ -z "$synapse_response" ] || [ -z "${synapse_response+x}" ]; then
            # If the response is not valid JSON or empty, treat it as an error
            echo "Error: Command returned empty or non-JSON response: $synapse_response"
            set -e
            rm "$stdout" "$stderr"
            return 1
        fi
        # Check if the 'admins' group is present
        exists=$(echo "$synapse_response" | jq '. | length')

        if [ "$exists" -gt 0 ]; then
           cat $stdout >&3
           set -e
           rm "$stdout" "$stderr"
           return 0
        fi
    fi
}

 if check_user_synapse_admin; then
 echo "UAMI already has Synapse Administrator role. No action needed"
 else
  echo -e "Creating Synapse workspace role assignment for User Assigned Managed Identity\n"
  execute_with_retry az synapse role assignment create \
    --workspace-name "$workspaceName" \
    --role "$synapseAdminRoleId" \
    --assignee-object-id "$userAssignedMIPrincipalId" \
    --scope "workspaces/$workspaceName"
  echo "Synapse Administrator role assigned successfully."
  sleep 5m
fi

if [ "$enableSox" == "true" ]; then
 echo "SOX is enabled. Adding Synapse User role for Compliance Monitoring SPN"
 execute_with_retry assign_synapse_role "$workspaceName" "Synapse User" "$complianceMonitoringSPNObjectId"
 echo "Synapse User role assigned successfully for Compliance Monitoring SPN"
fi

# Assign Synapse Monitoring Operator role to viewer group
echo -e "Creating Synapse workspace role assignment for Viewer Group\n"
execute_with_retry assign_synapse_role "$workspaceName" "Synapse Monitoring Operator" "$viewerGroupObjectId"

arguments.value: [replace(string(createObject('workspaceName', parameters('synapseWorkspaceName'), 'synapseAdminRoleId', __bicep.getRolesToIdsMap()['Synapse Administrator'], 'userAssignedMIPrincipalId', reference('userAssignedMI').principalId, 'subscriptionId', subscription().subscriptionId, 'resourceGroup', parameters('resourceGroupName'), 'viewerGroupObjectId', parameters('viewerGroupObjectId'), 'enableSox', parameters('enableSox'), 'complianceMonitoringSPNObjectId', parameters('complianceMonitoringSPNObjectId'))), '"', '\"')]

metadata.description: Instance name
UAMI id
Workspace name
Gloval cloud settings object containing environment suffixes and endpoints
Region where the workspace is deployed
Spark config name
Spark config description
Spark config object. Key value pairs of strings i.e. `{string:string}`.
Add default configs to the spark config. Adds `spark.dime_configstore_url` to the spark config.
UTC timestamp. Do not modify.
Resource group name.

variables.sparkConfigsScript:
# Fail script if any command fails
$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

# Assign script params to parameters object variable
$parameters = $args -join ' ' | ConvertFrom-Json -Depth 30

$subscriptionId = $parameters.subscriptionId
$computeWorkspaceName = $parameters.computeWorkspaceName
$configName = $parameters.sparkConfigName
$sparkConfig = $parameters.sparkConfig
$definitionFile = "$($configName).json"

# create definition file
$sparkConfig | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 -Compress | Out-File $definitionFile

# Set the specified subscription for the Azure CLI session
Set-AzContext -SubscriptionId $subscriptionId

# Install the Azure PowerShell module for Azure Synapse Analytics
Write-Host "Start install Az.Synapse module."
if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name Az.Synapse)) {
    Install-Module -Name Az.Synapse -AllowClobber -Force
}
Write-Host "Complete install Az.Synapse module."

# Load Az.Synapse Module
Write-Host "Start import Az.Synapse module."
Import-Module Az.Synapse
Write-Host "Complete import Az.Synapse module."

# Check if config already exists, if it exists update the config file
if (Get-AzSynapseSparkConfiguration -WorkspaceName $computeWorkspaceName | Where Name -eq $configName) {
    Write-Output "Spark configuration $configName exists, updating config file."

    # Export the existing spark configuration and overwrite the config file with the existing config
    Export-AzSynapseSparkConfiguration -WorkspaceName $computeWorkspaceName -Name $configName -OutputFolder  .
    
    # Read the existing spark configuration from the overwriten config file
    $oldSparkConfig = Get-Content -Path $definitionFile | ConvertFrom-Json -Depth 100

    # Update existing configs with the new configs
    $sparkConfig.configs.PSObject.Properties | ForEach-Object {
        $oldSparkConfig.Configs | Add-Member -NotePropertyName $_.Name -NotePropertyValue $_.Value -Force
    }

    # Update existing configs with the new configMergeRule
    $sparkConfig.configMergeRule.PSObject.Properties | ForEach-Object {
        $oldSparkConfig.ConfigMergeRule | Add-Member -NotePropertyName $_.Name -NotePropertyValue $_.Value -Force
    }

    # Write the updated configs to a file
    $oldSparkConfig | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 -Compress | Out-File $definitionFile
}

Write-Output "Updating $configName spark configuration in $computeWorkspaceName."

# Update the spark configuration
New-AzSynapseSparkConfiguration -WorkspaceName $computeWorkspaceName -DefinitionFile $definitionFile

# Get the updated configs, convert Json properties from pascalCase to camelCase
$updatedConfig = (Get-AzSynapseSparkConfiguration -WorkspaceName $computeWorkspaceName -Name $configName | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 -Compress)
$configsInCamelCase = [regex]::Replace(
    $updatedConfig, 
    '"([a-zA-Z])([a-zA-Z]+)":', 
    { "`"$($args[0].Groups[1].Value.ToLower())$($args[0].Groups[2].Value)`":" }
)

$DeploymentScriptOutputs = @{}
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['Name'] = $configName
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['Content'] = $configsInCamelCase

resources.name: [parameters('scriptName')]
resources.properties: [union(variables('versionProps'), createObject('scriptContent', parameters('scriptContent'), 'arguments', parameters('arguments'), 'cleanupPreference', parameters('cleanupPreference'), 'retentionInterval', parameters('retentionInterval'), 'forceUpdateTag', parameters('utcTime'), 'timeout', parameters('timeout')))]

metadata.description: Principal ID
Principal type

Details of identity to be assigned to the Kusto cluster

Gets a deployment safe name by hashing a given deployment name and returning a deterministic unique string less than or equal to 64 characters.
Example usage, `getSafeDeploymentName('my-deployment-name') => 'D4BF8FFBFB96424CA07EE1E3E7BCA12Emy-deployment-name'`
Name of the kusto cluster
Name of the kusto database
Location where the kusto cluster, database and tables will be deployed
The SKU of the Kusto Cluster
The tier of the Kusto Cluster
The min number of instances of the cluster
The max number of instances of the cluster
Enable all availability zones for the cluster. Default is `true`
The database viewers. Viewers can view tables in the database where `RestrictedViewAccess` isn't turned on. (Optional)
The cluster administrators. Admins can view and modify the entire Kusto cluster and databases. (Optional)
The cluster monitors. Monitors can view the entire Kusto cluster and its resources but can't modify it. (Optional)
Optional standalone tables to create in the Kusto Database without EventHub connections
Tags to apply to the resources
Indicates if the cluster is part of SOX enabled service
Choose Engine Type, uses V3 by default
Used to fetch the LA name for Kusto Cluster diagnostics (optional)
Compliance Monitoring SPN Application (Client) ID for SOX Kusto cluster monitoring

The max number of instances of the cluster
Enable all availability zones for the cluster
Public network access to the cluster is enabled by default. When disabled, only private endpoint connection to the cluster is allowed
List of allowed FQDNs(Fully Qualified Domain Name) for egress from Cluster
User assigned identities (optional)

resources.name: [parameters('name')]
resources.identity: [union(createObject('type', 'SystemAssigned'), if(greater(length(parameters('userAssignedIdentities')), 0), createObject('type', 'SystemAssigned, UserAssigned', 'userAssignedIdentities', toObject(parameters('userAssignedIdentities'), lambda('userAssignedIdentity', lambdaVariables('userAssignedIdentity')), lambda('_', createObject()))), createObject()))]
